samples.xml_description:
Implement a 16-bit divider, the dividend is 16-bit and the divider is 8-bit in combinational logic. Extract the higher bits of the dividend, matching the bit width of the divisor, and compare them with the divisor. If the dividend bits are greater, set the quotient to 1; otherwise, set it to 0 and use the difference as the remainder. Concatenate the remainder with the highest remaining 1-bit of the dividend and repeat the process until all dividend bits are processed. 16-bit dividend. 8-bit divisor. 16-bit quotient. 16-bit remainder. Number of bits to compare. Number of bits to shift. combinational_logic

Create two registers, a_reg and b_reg, to store the values of A and B.

 Register to store the value of A. Register to store the value of B.

Assign the value of A to a_reg.

Assign the value of B to b_reg.

Compare the upper M bits of a_reg and b_reg.

If a_reg[N:0] is greater than b_reg, set the quotient bit to 1 and subtract b_reg from a_reg.

Otherwise, set the quotient bit to 0.

Set the remainder to the difference.

Concatenate the remainder with the next bit of a_reg, and repeat the process until all bits of a_reg are processed.

Assign the result to the quotient and the odd to the remainder.

 Compare the upper M bits of a_reg and b_reg, if a_reg[N:0] is greater than b_reg, set the quotient bit to 1 and subtract b_reg from a_reg, otherwise set the quotient bit to 0 and set the remainder to the difference. Register to store the value of A. Register to store the value of B. The quotient bit. The remainder.

if a_reg[N:0] > b_reg, return 1 else return 0.
